title: Why do the Ultimate Kayak Trip

transcript.text: Hello, why do the UKT? When I tell people that I'm doing the UKT, they ask me some different
Questions. One of them is, why would you want to do that, kayak so far? And it's kind of
Seems to me like it's maybe in a context of like, it would be painful. Like, why would
You want to go and put yourself in pain? And my idea is that it's not going to be painful,
And my idea is that it'll be a whole barrel of laughs the whole time, like some kind of
Country club experience, like a vacation. And that's why it's so cool, because it's a way
To get out there and have an ultimate adventure and not spend a whole lot of money doing it.
So, yes, I did spend thousands and thousands of dollars with the equipment, but, you know,
I'm thinking that in the future, all this money worth of equipment won't be so expensive
As it is now. It'll be totally affordable, you know. Like, 20 years ago, mountain bikes
Were so expensive, but now you can get a bike that's just as good as the top of line, you
Know, $1,000 bike from 15 years ago. You can get now for like $300 or less. So, all the
Electronical stuff is going to be cheaper. So, the actual trip itself is quite cheap. And,
So personally, why I'm excited about doing it is there are lots of reasons. One of them
Is getting in shape. It's great to be in shape physically. You feel great. You don't have
To own anything. You don't have to know anybody. You don't have to have this, that, or the next
Thing. As long as you feel good physically, everything's great. You feel great. And you
Can think great, too, when you feel great. Like right now, I don't feel very good, because
I haven't been taking care of my body. So, I can't think very well. So, being in shape,
And, you know, I can't really self-motivate that well. So, it's kind of hard for me to
Be in shape. Unless I'm actually like, put myself in a shit. So, I'm going to put myself
In a shit. Get in shape. So, that's one reason. It's probably the main reason. Another reason
Is the vacation part of it. Yeah, the travel aspect of it. The UKT goes through maybe 25 or
Something different countries and principalities. I don't remember exactly, but it's a lot.
Maybe like 30 or something. So, I get to meet lots of different people. See lots of different
Things. There's lots of different islands. You have the flat ones. We've got the big mountain
Islands. And some of those, you see on Chile's islands, are really cool. There's like 25 different
Active volcanoes on there. There's Indians, the Carib Indians. I think they live on Dominica.
And, just the whole party atmosphere too. All these tourists. There's 50,000 people in
Cruise boats go by. One of those islands. I think most of them out there in the Eastern
Antilles. So, that'll be fun. And, you know, I kind of have trouble sleeping. I think it'd be
A great way to get to sleep. You know, if I'm paddling all day long, I'll sleep really
Well that night. Got a nice hammock. Let's see. What's another reason? Another reason? Another
Reason? It's this little thing I came up with. You know, like relaxing is great when you really
Need to relax because you've been pumping it all day long. And pumping it's really great
When you've been relaxing all the time. So, it's like you get to go from hanging out in the
Nice, pristine little island beach somewhere. Just kicking it. Plus, relaxing and hanging
Out, you know, sleeping in your hammock and stuff. And then you get to go out and paddle
Your brains out. And then, once you paddle your brains out, you get to go hang out again.
So, it's just these two extremes beating each other. Like this huge giant pendulum, you know. And
Once you're done with that whole trip, it's like, oh, I didn't waste my life. At least for that part
Of it. Now, another reason is that to do the UKT is I think it's good for the world. I really do think the UKT is good for the world.
I really do think the UKT is good for the world. Anything that motivates people to get out there and have fun
Without spending a lot of money, without being a strain on the resources is good. We really need to figure out a
Way to change the economy and not, you know, have an economy based on people using material things. And people
Then change that into economy where people are actually trading non-material things, things that don't stress the environment.

Trading knowledge as well. So, and, you know, I see a future where people will not, you know, the GDP will be strong enough so that people won't have to keep their job in order to keep their job, you know what I mean?
People will be able to take six months off instead of two weeks off, you know? Maybe it's only six months every few years, or at least you get your six every year.

I think that will be easier for people to do in the future. And, you know, I'm really not happy with how people are so afraid of the ocean. I was always afraid of the ocean as a child, and everyone would get the boat.

And I still am terrified of the ocean swimming in it. Probably a lot more than normal people. You know, people are too, but, you know, we're not going to be swimming in the ocean, we're going to be in a boat.

Sharks attack swimmers, but they don't attack kayaks, and if they do, it's going to swim away. So, you know, and people are afraid of the ocean, like, because your boat might sink, but, you know, my boat's not going to sink.
So, I'm not afraid of it. I really am. I'm not afraid. Maybe there's something out there that I'll notice when I get there. And if I do, then I'll be sure to share it with people.
But, you know, my common sense now says that, no, it's not dangerous. It's just a whole bunch of fun. Oh, and another reason is I get, it's the education aspect.
I'll have plenty of time to educate myself when I'm on UKT. When I'm paddling, I'll be able to listen to my MP3. I want to collect a lot of MP3 of stuff I download off of TED.com.
You can actually download those interviews. And I have a bunch of Coast to Coast. That's pretty good. And I think I'll buy some audiobooks, too.
I bought a bunch of e-books, but I got totally screwed. I bought them from e-books.com. And I downloaded PDF because my book reads PDF.
They have some kind of digital rights thing on there so that I can't actually put them on my e-book, my B-book.
So they totally ripped me off and I'm pissed off about it. And they're never going to hear the end of it until they get my, give me my 322 bags back.

Or 322 or 20 bucks dollars worth of books. Or let me, let me download my books again. But whatever. I got an e-book. It's called e-book. It's the best e-book out there because it's the only e-book out there that you can read HTML.
So I'll just copy and paste and throw HTML if I only want my e-book and I'll finish it to read. Stuff to read, sorry.
Um, so, you know, because if you live in a house in civilization, you just, there's too many distractions.
And for me, and I just can't sit down and read stuff. So, if I'm out in the middle of the ocean, or not in the middle of the ocean, but in the middle of some islands somewhere hanging out,
I'll have this stuff to read. Um, and it'll be great. I'll read and, oh no, I'll have to work out on the beach.
I'll have my little home exercises, trying to beat my records with those, which I'll have on my e-book.
And, uh, so that's why. That's why I'm doing the UK too. And, uh, I want to share it with people too. I think it's cool.
I want to motivate other people to do it, you know. I want to make waves in the world.
I know a lot of people say that kind of a thing. You know? Like, people are like, I want to be famous and all this kind of stuff.
And other people get angry with them for that. Like, is there ego or something like this?
I'm like, that's bad. And I'm sure it could be bad if that's like your goal, your number one goal.
But, you know, if you recognize it as a, as a, uh, another thing along, another reason, um, and you don't, you know, get your own ego into it, it's good to actually try to make waves.
We should all be trying to make waves in the world. We should all be trying to influence each other.
Um, being alive is not about locking yourself into a, in a cave, you know, or showing up at work and watching TV and not being a participant.
Being alive as a human being is about being a participant and becoming interested in something and trying to get other people to do it with you.

And, uh, trying to help people out that way. And so that's what it's all about. And, uh, so, you know, I'd like, I'd really want to see people, uh, getting out there and, uh, bike touring and not having to worry about, you know, passing through people's trust, uh, private property.

I think when people buy private property, they should buy it, you know, to be stewards of the lands to keep from developers from going in there, but not to keep from kayakers and mountain bikers and hikers from passing through their land.
You know, that's just bullshit. Dude, pisses me off.

But I don't think, uh, people who, like, say only Exynos are going to be like that. You know, I will definitely try to let everybody know that I'm passing through, but, um, you know, if somebody's like, screw you, this is my island, I don't want you here.
Obviously, I won't go, but, you know, I'm going to definitely talk about how that's pretty easy. Like in Chile, you can't go anywhere in the mountains because it's all private property, you know.
It's like, you can't come here in mine. Like, what? Um, so, why? Why do the kayak trip? To reiterate, hopefully I'm not over ten minutes, is exercise. Adventure.
Those are the three main reasons. Oh, yes, and then inspiration for. So, there you go. That's what I'm going to do.
